summary: I reviewed the iteration-4 requirements document (read from the matching `requirements-writer` step branch, since my `input.json` carried no resolved inputs) for completeness and atomicity, and issued a **pass** verdict in `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/requirements-completeness-critic/verdict.json`. All six findings from the previous iteration were verifiably addressed: both display surfaces are now enumerated with concrete placement (R1/R2), the pipeline state space is closed by explicit states plus a truthful catch-all (R10–R12), concurrent-step aggregation has a testable rule (R5/R6), the live-update guarantee is phrased as push-on-event with a latency bound (R14), and the iteration-number display matches the recorded clarifying-question default (R4). My own sweep found every user need from the ask traceable to a requirement, edge cases covered (out-of-order events, page-load truthfulness, accessibility without color-only status, cross-surface consistency), and each requirement written as a singular, observable when/then statement; the only remaining blemish — R2 redundantly restating liveness that R14/R15 already own — is stylistic and did not warrant blocking a fifth iteration, so the findings list is empty.
